title: Messy Vendor CSV Parser Architecture Bake-off

problem.statement: Hand-roll a Python parser for a messy vendor CSV dialect and determine which of two competing architectures provides the strongest objectively correct, robust, actionable, maintainable, constraint-adherent implementation.
problem.optimization_target: Maximize the weighted five-criterion score while treating objective sample tests and the correctness hard gate as authoritative, then synthesize a zero-regression champion from the winning architecture and only proven compatible helpers.
constraints: Do not import Python's standard-library csv module, pandas, or any third-party parser.
Handle quoted commas, embedded newlines, doubled-quote escaping, a BOM, comment lines, blank lines, ragged rows, and the specified whitespace rules.
Malformed input must raise an actionable ParseError with 1-based line and column information.
Run two line/regex Loops, two character-state-machine Loops, and two synthesis Loops using only the recorded model roles.
Embedded commas, embedded newlines, and doubled-quote escaping are blocking core samples.
success_criteria: Every blocking core sample passes and the primary objective correctness score reaches the hard gate of 5 points.
Shared architecture-neutral tests compare both finalists without favoring track-local test counts.
The independent qualitative judge breaks only objective parity and never overrides an objective failure.
The champion has runnable parser, tests, fixtures, clear ParseError evidence, complete prompt/feedback lineage, and verified artifact hashes.
problem.original_prompt:
Use the experiment-loop skill.

Hand-roll a parser for a messy vendor CSV dialect without importing the Python
standard-library `csv` module. It must handle quoted commas, embedded newlines,
doubled-quote escaping, a BOM, comment lines, blank lines, ragged rows, and
whitespace rules.

Score correctness, robustness, error clarity, maintainability, and constraint
adherence. Objective sample tests are primary. Embedded commas, embedded
newlines, and doubled-quote escaping are blocking core samples; a Track that
cannot pass one must be rejected. Malformed inputs must raise an actionable
`ParseError` with line and column information.

Run two competing architecture Tracks in parallel. Use `gpt-5.6-sol` for a
line-oriented/regular-expression approach and `claude-sonnet-5` for a
character-state-machine approach, with at least two Loops per Track. Use
`gpt-5.6-terra` as an independent maintainability and error-clarity judge; it
cannot override objective tests. Use `claude-opus-4.8` for a synthesis Track
that keeps the winning architecture and selectively incorporates any proven
helper from the rejected Track.

Produce runnable parser snapshots, committed sample fixtures, test-result
Artifacts, judge notes, complete prompt/feedback history, and a Manifest v1.1.
Record the actual model ID for every generator, judge, synthesis role, and Loop.

judges.role: provisional synthesis assessment subordinate to objective gates
independent maintainability and error-clarity judge; cannot override objective tests

tracks.label: Line-oriented / regular-expression architecture
tracks.hypothesis: Physical-line assembly plus anchored regular-expression field tokenization can robustly parse the vendor dialect while producing precise errors without becoming a whole-input CSV state machine.
tracks.final_decision: Promote loop 02: all 38 expanded objective tests and all blocking core gates pass.

tracks.label: Character-state-machine architecture
tracks.hypothesis: An explicit whole-input character state machine with named states, precise 1-based line/column tracking, and small per-state handlers will parse the messy vendor CSV dialect correctly (including all blocking core samples) while remaining easy to extend and giving highly actionable malformed-input errors, without importing csv/pandas/any third-party parser.
tracks.final_decision: new_best (within this Track); promoted loop-02 to final/ as the Track's champion snapshot for the later synthesis Track to compare against the line-oriented/regex Track.

tracks.label: Synthesis (keep winning architecture, selectively incorporate proven helpers)
tracks.hypothesis: Keeping the independent-judge-selected character-state-machine architecture verbatim, reconciling the strongest compatible objective evidence from both Tracks into one runnable suite, and incorporating only narrowly-proven architecture-neutral helpers from the rejected Track (while explicitly rejecting its scanner-duplicating regex/offset pipeline) yields a champion that is at least as correct as either finalist and clearer in its public error/misuse API, with no forbidden csv import.
tracks.final_decision: Promote synthesis-loop-02: 97/97 objective tests pass, all blocking core samples pass, forbidden-import scan passes, zero regression vs loop-01.

iterations.hypothesis: Regex physical-line splitting and field tokenization, with minimal quote-balance scanning, will cover the dialect and establish an objective baseline.
iterations.outcome: Ran 18 tests: 17 passed and 1 failed. All three blocking core samples passed, but post-closing-quote junk was reported at line 2, column 1 instead of the offending column 4, so the objective gate failed.

iterations.hypothesis: A single-pass character state machine (FIELD_START, IN_UNQUOTED, IN_QUOTED, QUOTE_PENDING, AFTER_QUOTE, IN_COMMENT states) driven by one while-loop with explicit line/column tracking can satisfy every dialect rule in the prompt on the first attempt.
iterations.outcome: All 30 objective tests passed, including all 3 blocking core samples (embedded comma, embedded newline, doubled-quote escaping). 15 additional ad-hoc adversarial probes (empty quoted pairs, quoted-field flush at EOF, odd-trailing-quote unterminated detection, multi-line error position tracking, bare-CR-in-quotes, BOM in multiple positions, mixed line endings, unicode content, etc.) also behaved correctly. No functional defects were found. Self-review flagged a genuine maintainability weakness instead: parse() was one ~250-line procedural function with the position-advance pattern (pos += 1; col += 1) duplicated roughly 15 times and mutable buffers manipulated inline rather than through an encapsulated cursor abstraction.

prompt.track_prompt: Hand-roll a robust Python parser for a messy vendor CSV dialect without importing the standard-library csv module... This is Loop 1 of 2: baseline architecture and objective tests.
prompt.input_feedback: None -- first loop of the Track.
prompt.judge_feedback: correctness 5/5, robustness 4/5, error_clarity 4/5, maintainability 2/5, constraint_adherence 5/5. All objective tests and blocking core samples pass; the material weakness is architectural (monolithic function, duplicated advance logic), not a correctness/robustness defect.
prompt.next_prompt: Refactor to fix the maintainability weakness (extract a Scanner class owning pos/line/col with advance/advance_newline helpers; give each state its own handler method) without changing observable behavior. Promote the 15 ad-hoc adversarial probes into committed tests and add more, focusing on error-location precision.

lesson.trigger: Self-review plus 15 ad-hoc adversarial probes found zero functional defects but revealed a single ~250-line procedural parse() with the pos+=1/col+=1 advance pattern duplicated roughly 15 times.
lesson.action: Extract position/line/column tracking into a dedicated Scanner class and split the state dispatch into one handler method per named state before adding more features or tests.
lesson.evidence: loop-01/judge.md maintainability score 2/5 with the specific duplication called out; confirmed no behavior depended on the duplication (loop-02's refactor kept all 30 original tests passing unchanged).

iterations.hypothesis: Extracting a _Scanner class (sole owner of pos/line/col) and replacing the monolithic dispatch with one handler method per _State will fix loop-01's maintainability weakness with zero behavioral regression, and promoting/expanding adversarial and error-location tests will raise robustness and error_clarity confidence from 'probed informally' to 'proven by committed regression tests'.
iterations.outcome: Refactor completed: _Scanner (advance/advance_newline) + _Machine with 6 per-state handler methods (_step_field_start, _step_in_unquoted, _step_in_quoted, _step_quote_pending, _step_after_quote, _step_in_comment) dispatched via a dict. ParseError now also reports row_number/field_number. test_parser.py grew from 30 to 53 tests (all 30 loop-01 cases kept verbatim + 23 new, 12 promoted from loop-01's ad-hoc probes and 11 newly written), all 53 passing, all 3 blocking core samples passing. Regression check: loop-01's original, unmodified test_parser.py was run against loop-02's parser.py in a throwaway directory and passed 30/30, confirming zero behavioral regression.

iterations.hypothesis: Preserving the winning state machine verbatim, committing one runnable union suite (winning 53 + local shared bakeoff 31), and adding only the rejected Track's non-string boundary guard will keep correctness at 5/5 while improving misuse diagnostics, without touching the architecture or importing csv.

prompt.track_prompt: Run the synthesis Track: keep the winning character-state-machine architecture, reconcile the strongest compatible test/API evidence from both Tracks, incorporate only narrowly-proven architecture-compatible helpers, never import csv, preserve the public API and dialect rules. loop-01: establish a fresh synthesis candidate and run the state-machine suite plus a synthesis-local copy of the shared suite. Multi-parent lineage [line-regex-loop-02, track-state-machine-loop-02].
prompt.input_feedback: Winning base = track-state-machine-loop-02 (53/53, Scanner + per-state handlers, ParseError with line/col/row/field). Rejected = line-regex-loop-02 (38/38, ValueError-based ParseError, offset/regex pipeline, non-string guard). Independent gpt-5.6-terra judge: winner 4.940 vs 4.745, proven_helpers_for_synthesis = [] because the regex offset/record pipeline would duplicate the scanner.
prompt.judge_feedback: 87/87 pass; blocking samples pass; forbidden-import scan pass; weighted 4.85. Accepted exactly one helper (non-string guard, evidence cited); rejected the regex/offset pipeline and the ValueError re-base. Union testing found no functional bug; surfaced a deliberate Unicode-whitespace edge.
prompt.next_prompt: loop-02: improve only from this loop's real evidence with behavior-safe changes and dedicated regression tests -- expose ParseError.reason and pin the deliberate ASCII-only whitespace behavior; add runnable fixtures; do not manufacture a failure.

lesson.trigger: The independent judge recorded zero architecture-compatible helpers from the rejected Track, yet the rejected Track carried a small, proven, architecture-neutral API idea (non-string guard) not covered by the shared suite.
lesson.action: Incorporate only the boundary-level guard (with cited evidence) and reject the scanner-duplicating regex/offset pipeline; commit the union of both Tracks' objective evidence locally to prove shared-suite compatibility.
lesson.evidence: 87/87 pass; the non-string guard converts an opaque internal TypeError from slicing None into an actionable 'text must be str'; union testing revealed only an under-specified (not incorrect) Unicode-whitespace edge.

iterations.hypothesis: Because loop-01 found no functional bug, two measured behavior-safe changes driven by loop-01 evidence -- exposing ParseError.reason and pinning the winner's deliberate ASCII-only whitespace behavior -- will raise API/documentation clarity with zero regression, and runnable fixtures will demonstrate the champion end-to-end.
iterations.outcome: 97/97 objective tests pass (87 inherited + 4 ParseError.reason + 3 Unicode-whitespace pinning + 3 fixture loading). Zero regression: loop-01's unmodified 87-test suite passes against loop-02's parser.py. All blocking core samples pass; forbidden-import scan passes. Message text and existing ParseError attributes are unchanged; the only runtime-visible addition is the .reason attribute.

Under-specified Unicode-whitespace edge pinned; fixtures exercised end-to-end.
ParseError.reason adds programmatic access to the cause on top of line/column/row/field.
Same clean Scanner + per-state design; handlers still mutate shared state (documented future work).

prompt.track_prompt: loop-02: improve only from actual loop-01 evidence (a real gap found by union testing/review, error/API clarity, or maintainability); if no functional bug exists, make a measured, behavior-safe improvement with a dedicated regression test. parent_ids [synthesis-loop-01]. Do not manufacture failures. Include runnable sample fixtures under loop-02/fixtures. Keep the winning architecture; never import csv; preserve the public API and dialect rules.
prompt.input_feedback: loop-01: 87/87 pass, no functional bug. Evidence: (1) the winner exposes structured line/col/row/field but not the bare cause; (2) union testing surfaced a deliberate, untested Unicode-whitespace edge.
prompt.judge_feedback: 97/97 pass; two behavior-safe improvements with dedicated regression tests (ParseError.reason; Unicode-whitespace pinning). Zero regression proven (87/87). All hard gates green; weighted 4.85. Champion.
prompt.next_prompt: No loop-03 (plan = two Loops). If maintainability becomes decisive, reshape each _step_* handler into a pure (state, char) -> (new_state, actions) function; otherwise acquire real vendor fixtures to resolve the Unicode-whitespace question against ground truth.

lesson.trigger: loop-01 found no functional defect, so an improvement had to be genuinely additive rather than a manufactured bug fix.
lesson.action: Expose ParseError.reason (message and other attributes unchanged) and commit regression tests pinning the winner's deliberate ASCII-only whitespace behavior, plus runnable fixtures.
lesson.evidence: 97/97 pass and a zero-regression re-run of loop-01's unmodified 87-test suite against loop-02's parser.py confirmed no observable behavior change beyond the new attribute.

iterations.stop_reason: Synthesis Track plan called for exactly two Loops; loop-02 delivered two measured, behavior-safe, evidence-driven improvements with zero regression and all hard gates green. Promoted to final/ as the synthesis champion.

champion.summary: The final synthesis keeps the independently selected character-state-machine architecture, adds only the proven architecture-neutral non-string guard plus additive error API/documentation improvements, and passes 97/97 objective tests with zero regression.

reasons.text: The champion passes 97/97 objective tests, every blocking core sample, the forbidden-import scan, and the 87-test zero-regression rerun.
Its promoted parser provides actionable ParseError reason, line, column, row, and field context while preserving the explicit Scanner and named-state architecture.

caveats.text: Maintainability remains 4/5 because per-state handlers coordinate through mutable machine buffers; a pure transition-function refactor is deferred.
Unicode-only structural whitespace remains under-specified by the prompt, although the champion now pins its deliberate ASCII-only interpretation with regression tests.

milestones.caption: Line/regex baseline passed every blocking sample but was rejected when its primary objective location assertion failed.
Source-offset mapping repaired the measured diagnostic failure and produced a 38/38 finalist.
The state-machine baseline was correct but exposed a monolithic-function maintainability weakness.
A Scanner and per-state handlers delivered zero regression, 53/53 tests, and the independent judge's preferred base.
Multi-parent synthesis preserved the winning architecture, added one boundary guard, and passed an 87-test union suite.
Evidence-driven API and contract improvements reached 97/97 with zero regression and were promoted as champion.

rules: Objective tests are authoritative: the primary correctness criterion is a hard gate at >=5 points, and a primary objective failure forces rejection regardless of qualitative opinion.
Embedded commas, embedded newlines, and doubled-quote escaping are blocking core samples; any failure blocks promotion.
The independent gpt-5.6-terra judge may break an objective tie on maintainability and error clarity but may never override objective tests.
Track-local test counts are supporting evidence only; the shared architecture-neutral bakeoff is the primary cross-track comparison.
A synthesis helper from the rejected architecture is accepted only when objective evidence shows it is architecture-compatible and non-duplicative.
synthesis: The line/regex baseline was correctly rejected when Loop 01 failed its primary objective location test despite passing all core samples; Loop 02 repaired that failure and entered the shared bakeoff. Both finalists then passed all 64 shared tests and blocking samples. A blind-first-pass gpt-5.6-terra qualitative tie-break selected the character-state-machine base (4.940 vs 4.745) without overriding objective parity. Synthesis preserved that architecture, accepted only the line/regex track's architecture-neutral non-string guard, explicitly rejected its duplicate regex/offset pipeline, added ParseError.reason and pinned whitespace behavior, and promoted synthesis-loop-02 after 97/97 tests, all hard gates, and an 87/87 zero-regression rerun passed. Remaining caveats are mutable handler state and under-specified Unicode whitespace.
